title: Amendment to Executive Order 13603 Regarding Defense Production Act Authority Delegations
summary: This executive order amends Executive Order 13603 from March 16, 2012, which addresses National Defense Resources Preparedness. The order modifies certain delegations of Presidential authority under the Defense Production Act. The Defense Production Act grants the President broad powers to direct the production and allocation of resources for national defense purposes. This order adjusts which executive branch officials and agencies are delegated specific authorities under this act, potentially redistributing responsibility for implementing defense resource controls and priorities among different departments and agencies.

title: Executive Order on Advertising Standards for Products Labeled as Made in America
summary: This executive order establishes policy requiring that products advertised as Made in America must be substantiated as actually manufactured in the United States. The order directs that American consumers have access to clear and accurate information about the origin of products bearing Made in America claims. The executive order addresses the protection of consumers against misleading advertising practices related to domestic manufacturing claims.

title: Executive Order on Regulatory Streamlining for Residential Housing Construction
summary: This executive order directs the federal government to identify and remove regulatory requirements and restrictions that impede the construction and renovation of residential housing. The order establishes that housing market dynamism depends on maintaining diverse inventory levels of newly constructed and renovated homes annually. The order addresses what the administration characterizes as unnecessary regulatory layers that may affect housing development and construction activities.

title: Executive Order on Mortgage Credit Access and Lending Standards
summary: This executive order directs federal agencies to establish policies and procedures aimed at expanding access to mortgage credit for American homebuyers. The order establishes the objective that individuals seeking to purchase homes should have access to mortgages from reliable lenders at interest rates that reflect their creditworthiness. The order addresses lending practices and credit availability in the residential mortgage market, tasking relevant federal agencies with implementing measures to facilitate mortgage lending while maintaining lending standards.

title: Proclamation on Women's History Month, 2026
summary: A presidential proclamation designating March 2026 as Women's History Month. The proclamation recognizes the historical and contemporary contributions of American women across various sectors including family, culture, defense, industry, and national development. The document acknowledges women's roles in strengthening American institutions and shaping the nation's development over a 250-year period.

title: Presidential Nomination and Withdrawal Notices Sent to Senate
summary: Notice of presidential actions sent to the United States Senate regarding personnel appointments and withdrawals. Sarah B. Rogers of New York has been nominated to serve as Chief Executive Officer of the United States Agency for Global Media. Additionally, the withdrawal of William Hewes III of Mississippi has been sent to the Senate, who had been nominated to serve as a Commissioner of the Consumer Product Safety Commission for a seven-year term beginning October 27, 2024.

title: Presidential Memorandum on Compensation Practices at the Tennessee Valley Authority
summary: This Presidential Memorandum directs the Board of Directors of the Tennessee Valley Authority to implement measures related to compensation practices. Issued under the authority granted to the President by the Constitution and the Tennessee Valley Authority Act of 1933, the memorandum addresses fiscal matters concerning employee compensation and related personnel policies at the TVA, a federal agency responsible for regional development, power generation, and resource management in the Tennessee Valley region.

title: Presidential Nominations for Cabinet and Federal Positions
summary: This document contains nominations submitted to the United States Senate for various federal positions. Markwayne Mullin of Oklahoma has been nominated for the position of Secretary of Homeland Security. James Arnott of Missouri has been nominated to serve as United States Marshal for the Western District of Missouri for a four-year term. Jack Chambers of West Virginia has been nominated to serve as United States Marshal for the Southern District of West Virginia for a specified term. These nominations require Senate confirmation before the individuals can assume their respective offices.

title: Proclamation Establishing U.S. Hostage and Wrongful Detainee Day, 2026
summary: A presidential proclamation designating a day to recognize Americans held captive or wrongfully detained abroad. The proclamation acknowledges individuals subjected to extended confinement and expresses commitment to efforts related to their situations.
